interpretation: Temperature-10 soft ratios are closer to one than hard ratios, so smoothing contributes to the apparent hard-threshold excess.
Temperature-10 soft ratios still substantially exceed one at long lead in both seeds, so the evaluation surrogate mismatch is not the primary cause.
Sharper diagnostic temperatures approach the hard ratios; they do not show that the frozen model is already calibrated under a sharper surrogate.
The next hypothesis is autoregressive exposure mismatch because training ends at 92 percent teacher forcing while validation uses zero.
caveat: The original audit used a broadcast tensor comparison for hard counts. Exact official hard ratios here come from the frozen LeadTimeVILMetrics outputs; the script has been corrected to reproduce that scalar comparison in future runs.
